prompt: The interface method is not public, causing a compile error when implemented. What is the bug?
choices: Interface methods cannot be overridden in implementing classes
Interface methods are implicitly public; the implementing class must also declare print() as public — using package-private (no modifier) is a narrower access and causes a compile error
void return type must be changed to String in the implementing class
explanation: Interface methods are implicitly public abstract. Overriding methods cannot have weaker access. Doc.print() with no modifier is package-private (weaker than public), causing compile error: 'attempting to assign weaker access privileges'. Fix: add public to Doc.print().
